run_summary: Screen 2 has two open positions with earnings imminent: MOD prints tomorrow (T+1 window closing), GTLB prints in 8 calendar days (still in holding window). Three new flags arrived, all OVERDONE or marginal UNDERDONE; only MDB passes the BUY bar cleanly. Track record is poor (0% on UNDERDONE, 60% on OVERDONE), suggesting conservative bias may be warranted on borderline calls.

entries.name: GitLab
entries.thesis: The combination of (1) a pre-print 'in-line only' guidance reaffirmation, (2) a same-day workforce reduction, and (3) clear deceleration in net retention (123%→118%) and widening net loss in the just-closed FY26 suggests the filings are flagging more downside risk than a SaaS multiple typically prices. RIF + reaffirm is rarely a setup for an upside surprise on a name where the bull case requires AI-driven re-acceleration.
entries.what_kills: Q1 beat with a guidance raise (would imply the May 11 reaffirmation was a sandbag); NRR stabilizing or ticking up; explicit Duo Agent Platform credit/usage metrics that show monetization traction; the RIF framed as pure reinvestment with revenue line untouched.
entries.reasoning: Classification: OVERDONE. Long-only book has no short mechanic and cannot express OVERDONE. This is a recorded valid Screen 2 signal (classification is OVERDONE with confidence 3, filings evidence is concrete: pre-announcement reaffirm + RIF + NRR decel 123%→118% + widening net loss), but the signal cannot be traded in this portfolio structure. OVERDONE — long-only book has no way to express this.

entries.name: Victoria's Secret & Co.
entries.thesis: Filings show a quantified, ongoing cost headwind ($85M FY25 op income hit from tariffs) layered on top of a still-shrinking store fleet and a leadership team that turned over its CEO, CFO/COO, and CMO within ~12 months. Q1 is structurally the weakest quarter for an intimates retailer and is the first one fully exposed to the new Section 122 10% global tariff layered over a leadership team that just took the seat. The combination raises the probability of either a Q1 miss or — more likely — a cautious FY26 guide that the market is under-pricing.
entries.what_kills: Mitigation actions (pricing, sourcing shifts) already in place show up as in-line gross margin; management quantifies a smaller-than-expected FY26 tariff impact; Q1 comps positive on a clean basis.
entries.reasoning: Classification: OVERDONE. Long-only book cannot express short signals. Although the filings evidence is detailed (quantified $85M tariff FY25 hit, new 10% Section 122 tariff, leadership turnover, store count decline, Q1 weakness ahead), the classification is OVERDONE and therefore cannot be held in a long-only portfolio. OVERDONE — long-only book has no way to express this.
